# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json
COPY package*.json ./

# Install app dependencies
RUN npm install

# Bundle app source inside Docker image
COPY . .

# Build the production bundle
RUN npm run build

# Use an official Nginx runtime as a parent image for serving the app
FROM nginx:alpine

# Copy the built app to the Nginx default directory
COPY --from=0 /usr/src/app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose the port the app runs on
EXPOSE 80

# Start Nginx
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
